---
title: "Notebooks"
---

The notebooks are student-facing companions to the slide decks. They are designed to be run, modified, and rerun.

Use the Google Colab links if you want to work in a browser without installing Python locally.

| Week | Topic | Notebook | Google Colab |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 1 | Image formation as arrays | [Download](week01_image_formation.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week01_image_formation.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week01_image_formation.ipynb) |
| 2 | Convolution and blur | [Download](week02_convolution_blur.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week02_convolution_blur.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week02_convolution_blur.ipynb) |

## Working Locally

From the repository root:

```bash
python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt
jupyter notebook
```

The notebooks use Plotly for interactive figures, so they are especially useful for changing parameters and immediately seeing the effect.
